Good evening, With housing prices rising, healthcare costs increasing and worries about savings escalating, many Americans are pondering a move abroad. A new report ranks countries offering amenities like lower living costs, tax benefits or English-speaking communities. Find out which countries made the list here. Thanks for reading! 73637.subject: рЯФЈ The Daily: UK sanctions HTX
73637.body:
Robinhood clears final regulatory approval for WonderFi deal | Strive leapfrogs Coinbase, Riot with latest $85.4 million bitcoin buy | Hyperliquid rolls out validator-governed prediction markets for real-world events | Nathan Allman, founder and CEO of Ondo Finance, dies unexpectedly |

The Daily ( https://www.theblock.co/?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters )

Written by James Hunt | Edited by Daniel Kuhn

Presented by

LMAX ( https://www.theblock.co/lmax?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters )

Happy Tuesday! *Bitcoin's* latest wobble below $77,000 reflects a market growing increasingly sensitive to Iran-related geopolitical headlines, according to analysts ( https://www.theblock.co/post/402552/optimism-looks-fragile-bitcoin-wavers-as-iran-strikes-revive-geopolitical-tensions-and-analysts-warn-of-range-trap?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters ) , with rising leverage and underwater buyers risking a prolonged $72,000-$82,000 range unless fresh institutional demand returns.

In today's newsletter, the *UK* sanctions *HTX* over support for *Russia* , *Robinhood* clears the final regulatory approval for its acquisition of *WonderFi* , *Ondo Finance's* CEO dies unexpectedly, and more.

Meanwhile, *TeraWulf* stock jumps ( https://www.theblock.co/post/402616/terawulf-stock-jumps-12-news-kentucky-data-center-site-acquisition?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters ) over 12% on news of its Kentucky data center site acquisition.

*P.S.* Don't forget to check out The Funding , a biweekly rundown of crypto VC trends. It's a great read вАФ and just like The Daily , it's free to subscribe ( https://www.theblock.co/newsletters?select=The%20Funding&utm_campaign=website&utm_campaign=website&utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_medium=referral&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=newsletter&utm_source=newsletter&utm_source=newsletter&utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters ) !

*THE BIG ONE*

*рЯЗђрЯЗІ UK sanctions HTX over support of Russia in broad sweep over crypto exchanges*

( https://www.theblock.co/post/402591/uk-sanctions-htx-over-support-of-russia-in-broad-sweep-over-crypto-exchanges?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters )

The *UK* sanctioned crypto exchange *HTX* , formerly known as *Huobi* , accusing the platform of supporting the *Russian government* through financial services linked to sanctions evasion.

* British authorities specifically alleged HTX provided financial services or economic resources to Russian-linked firms, including *A7 LLC* and *Garantex Europe OU*.
* The sanctions form part of a broader UK crackdown targeting crypto exchanges, banks, and companies accused of helping Russia evade international sanctions.
* Blockchain analytics firm *Elliptic* said the move marks the first time the UK has used Regulation 17

 The Economist

Patrick Lane

Senior digital editor



 As the tenth anniversary of the Brexit vote draws near, Britain has in some ways quietly 
 
https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ4ODh9AAcAAAAAAeSCGsXyC8gEwLlmQncFpRs6qUNw7oaDhtKEpyymCMFAfCy1ZIv1iepgZCmiBIt72MlShvN5n_e1mPqp1gUa-i3c15FWVFAY899B-Rgm 
realigned itself 
 with the European Union. But there are big obstacles, both financial and political, in the way of a closer relationship.

рЯІ† AIвАЩs Crypto News Brief: Markets enter cautious consolidation as Bitcoin volatility hits historic lows. While institutional accumulation from firms like Strive persists, heavy ETF outflows signal cooling retail demand. Regulatory clarity via the CLARITY Act remains the primary catalyst. рЯУЙBitcoin Volatility Hits 9-Month Low Amid $77K Consolidation BitcoinвАЩs expected volatility has dropped to its lowest level since 2023 as the asset struggles to break the $80,000 resistance. Market analysts point to $1 billion in monthly ETF outflows as a sign of temporary cooling in retail demand. Read full story рЯТ∞Strive Asset Management Adds 1,109 Bitcoin to Treasury Strive (ASST) has increased its total holdings to 16,500 BTC, positioning itself as the seventh-largest public corporate holder globally. The firm continues a strategy of aggressive accumulation to provide shareholders with direct digital asset exposure. See details рЯПЫпЄПDigital Asset Market Clarity Act Advances Through Senate The CLARITY Act has successfully passed the Senate Banking Committee, moving closer to a final July 4 deadline. The legislation aims to unify US crypto oversight and provide the regulatory certainty needed for multi-trillion dollar institutional entries. Read full story рЯЪ®White House Executive Order Mandates Regulatory Reassessment President Trump signed a directive ordering federal agencies to eliminate outdated barriers hindering digital asset integration. The order specifically targets the removal of restrictive accounting rules and encourages bank-grade crypto custody. See details рЯТОBitmine Immersion Holdings Reach 4.4% of Total Ether Supply Bitmine (BMNR) now holds 5.39 million ETH, valued at over $10 billion, following a massive staking-focused accumulation drive. The firm's total crypto and cash reserves have reached a milestone of $12.3 billion. Read full story рЯЪАNEAR Protocol Surges 15% on AI-Native Blockchain Upgrades Defying a broader market dip, NEAR Protocol hit a six-month high driven by anticipation of the v2.13 network upgrade. Investors are increasingly positioning in NEAR due to its specialized architecture for high-performance AI operations. See details рЯУКCrypto Funds Record $1.47 Billion in Weekly Outflows Global digital asset funds experienced their third-largest outflow of 2026 as investors pulled capital from major Bitcoin pro
